name: Caramelized Onion Confit
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the olive oil and butter in a large heavy-bottomed saucepan over medium heat.
2. When the butter foams, add half of the sliced onions and 2 bay leaves. Sprinkle with half of the sugar and salt.
3. Add remaining onions, bay leaves, sugar, and salt. Cover and reduce heat to low. Cook for 20 minutes.
4. Uncover, stir, and increase heat to medium. Cook for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. Increase heat to high and cook, stirring constantly, until the onions are a deep golden brown, about 10 minutes.
6. Add the wine and stir, scraping the bottom of the pan to dislodge any browned bits. Cook for 4 or 5 minutes longer.
Delicious with roast meats or poultry, as well as leftover turkey sandwiches (try topping with a little cranberry sauce). Makes about 3 cups.
